NavAutopilot: fix route-edit loop via capture state reset and heading freeze

Fix A (ActLegsView): call nav.ResetCaptureState() after CommitRoute so stale
wasNear/nearFrames/prevDist state from the previous leg does not immediately
trigger a spurious waypoint advance after a route rebuild.

Fix B (NavAutopilot): freeze targetHdgDeg to the aircraft's current yaw when
waypoints are empty/null and NAV is engaged, preventing a stale intercept
command from being held during the coroutine-based rebuild gap.

Also adds NavAutopilot.ResetCaptureState() public method.

commit.message:
Phase 2 fix: merge left+right hands before tokenization

REMI is a single-track format. Previously, split_files_for_training
processed each MIDI track independently, producing separate right-hand
and left-hand chunks. The model fine-tuned on those learned single-hand
patterns and generated single-hand output.

Fix: merge all tracks into one before chunking and seeding.
- Added merge_tracks() utility in setup cell
- All 19 corpus MIDIs now produce two-hand chunks (58 train, 5 valid)
- Lost waltz seed is now 1 merged sequence (1148 tokens, was 2x separate)
- Reduced pitch augmentation from ±12 to ±3/±5 semitones
- Added data/chunked/ and data/merged/ to .gitignore (regenerable)

commit.message:
fix: critical memory safety issue in HttpClient.formatBytes

CRITICAL BUG FIX — Memory Safety Violation

Root cause: formatBytes() returned pointer to stack-allocated buffer
- Function used local var buf: [32]u8 = undefined
- Returned slice pointing to this stack memory
- Caused undefined behavior when buffer went out of scope
- Violated core library principle: no memory safety issues

Fix: Proper allocator-based memory management
- Changed formatBytes to accept Allocator parameter
- Use allocPrint instead of bufPrint for heap allocation
- Caller now owns returned memory and must free it
- Added proper defer statements for cleanup

Impact:
- Prevents potential crashes and corruption
- Ensures memory safety in download stats display
- All tests passing, cross-platform builds verified

Severity: HIGH — Library code must never have UB

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
ci: install golangci-lint via go install to match project Go version

Pre-built golangci-lint binaries are compiled with go1.24, but
go.mod requires go1.25+. Installing via go install compiles the
linter with the project's own toolchain, eliminating the version
mismatch. Also runs go mod tidy which bumped go directive to 1.25.0
(required by a transitive dependency).

commit.message:
回測引擎新增 20+ 專業量化指標（Sortino, Calmar, VaR, Hurst 等）

在 runBacktest() 加入完整機構級量化指標計算：
- 風險調整報酬：Sortino, Calmar, Omega
- 風險指標：VaR 95%/99%, CVaR, 年化波動率
- 統計特徵：Skewness, Kurtosis, Hurst Exponent, Tail Ratio
- 交易品質：Payoff Ratio, EV, Recovery Factor
- 持倉分析：最大連勝/連敗, 平均持有天數
- 痛苦指標：Ulcer Index, Pain Ratio

回測結果 UI 升級為分類儀表板（6 類 20+ 指標）

  en vez de '1','2','3' que colisionaban con el item default

  evitar mezcla number/string que causa que el browser no actualice

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix natural-mode crop to output at native bounding-box dimensions

Previously, natural mode scaled the cropped region back up to 1024px wide,
making the output the same size as the source — no visible crop. Now the
output canvas is exactly srcW × srcH (the tight bounding box of non-transparent
pixels), producing a genuinely smaller image when the object doesn't fill the
full source canvas.

Square mode is unchanged: content is letterboxed inside canvasWidth × canvasWidth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add pre-flight skill and extend pr-review with architectural awareness (#9)

* Add pre-flight skill and extend pr-review with architectural awareness

- Add pre-flight skill: developer self-check before raising a PR, surfaces
  observations as questions (not verdicts), writes log to .pre-flight/ for
  pattern discovery over time
- Update pr-review: checks for pre-flight log on the branch, notes absence
  once without blocking; expands guideline doc search to include
  docs/architecture/ and docs/*-guidelines.md
- Add docs/achitecture-intent.md: intent document for the architecture review
  approach

rationale explaining why the pre-flight skill works the way it does.

that's pure governance and needs no LLM.

commit.message:
Fix: strip markdown code fences from AI-generated output

AI agents sometimes wrap generated code in markdown fences
(e.g. \`\`\`html ... \`\`\` or \`\`\`tsx ... \`\`\`). Strip
opening and closing fences before opening the result in the editor,
for both complete and partial (cancelled/error) outputs.

commit.message:
Fix elevation ramp: incline holds flat to boundary, then slopes into next

The ramp was straddling the interval boundary (starting before, ending
after). Now the flat segment holds the current incline all the way to
the boundary, then the ramp slopes into the next interval over a short
distance (capped at 30% of the next interval width).

the required toolchain, matching the fix already applied to the
Dockerfile.

commit.message:
fix: prevent widget audio copies from appearing in My Files

The media browser scanned the audio/ prefix for My Files, but that
prefix is used internally by AudioStorageService to store copies of
sounds added to music widget slots. User uploads go to uploads/.

commit.message:
fix(issues): fix infinite re-render loop in IssuesScreen

Zustand selector returning an object literal creates a new reference on every
render, causing Zustand to trigger another update → infinite loop.
Split into two separate scalar selectors to avoid the issue.

commit.message:
fix: Mermaid — JS пост-рендер для автоматического контраста текста

CSS-подход не работал из-за конфликта cascade: base.scss задаёт
p,text { color/fill: var(--darkgray) } что побеждает Mermaid per-node styles.

Решение: после mermaid.run() итерируем по нодам, проверяем luminance
фона (WCAG формула), принудительно ставим #fff или #1a1a1a на text/p.

Функция isColorDark(): hex (#rgb, #rrggbb), rgb(), rgba() → bool.
